reasoning: The ICE BofA Single-A US Corporate Index OAS experienced a sharp spike late in February 2025, rising from 0.66 around Feb 20 to 0.75 on Feb 28. This value represents the nearest approximation to the March 2 cutoff value (since March 2 is a Sunday and not a month-end, the Friday close serves as the most recent baseline). Statistical analysis over the last 30 days indicates an underlying trend line near 0.70, making the 0.75 print an acute short-term deviation (+3 standard deviations above the local trend). Corporate bond spreads typically exhibit asymmetric behavior where sharp intraday or weekly spikes tend to rapidly retrace unless driven by a systemic recessionary trigger. Since we are observing a short-term risk-off event, mean reversion strongly favors a retrace back towards ~0.70 in the immediate term. Consequently, there is a relatively high likelihood that the spread will be below 0.75 over the 1-week and 1-month horizons, so probabilities are assigned below 0.5 initially. Over time, standard variance increases as broader macroeconomic conditions overpower the localized short-term technicals. In strict accordance with the forecasting rules, uncertainty must increase over time—thus, the probabilities gracefully approach 0.50 (maximum uncertainty) for the longer horizons.
